senses.definition: A very important or leading beacon or signal, especially used figuratively for someone seen as a strong example or guide.
examples: She became the archbeacon of reform in the organization, lighting the way for change.
The scientist is hailed as an archbeacon of climate awareness in his country.

senses.definition: A chief or senior beadle, especially in older or humorous use, referring to an official who keeps order in a church, college, or court.
examples: The archbeadle marched solemnly at the head of the procession.
In the novel, an archbeadle bullies the poor while flattering the rich.

senses.definition: A bishop of the highest rank, who is in charge of the churches and other bishops in a large area or province of the Christian Church.
examples: The archbishop led the service in the packed cathedral.
He was appointed archbishop of the largest diocese in the country.
The archbishop met with community leaders to discuss social issues.

senses.definition: A rare or old-fashioned word for a female archbishop, or sometimes for the wife of an archbishop.
examples: The author imagined an archbishopess who defied the customs of her time.
In some older texts, the archbishopess is mentioned only briefly.

senses.definition: The area, office, or authority of an archbishop.
examples: The archbishopric covers several major cities and rural regions.
He held the archbishopric for more than twenty years.
The dispute concerned the boundaries of the archbishopric.

senses.definition: Plural of archbishopric: several areas, offices, or authorities held by archbishops.
examples: Over time, new archbishoprics were created as the church expanded.
The council discussed how the archbishoprics should be reorganized.

senses.definition: Another word for archbishopric, meaning the position or area of authority of an archbishop.
examples: He inherited the archbishopry during a time of political unrest.
The archbishopry became a centre of learning and culture.

senses.definition: Plural of archbishop: several bishops of the highest rank who lead large church areas or provinces.
examples: The conference brought together archbishops from around the world.
Two archbishops spoke about peace and reconciliation.

senses.definition: Someone who is extremely bad at doing a job, especially one who spoils work through carelessness or lack of skill.
examples: The repairs were so poor that he was called an archbotcher.
Only an archbotcher could have ruined such a simple task.

senses.definition: A very rare word meaning a chief instigator of trouble, violence, or war.
examples: The diplomat warned that an archboutefeu was pushing both sides towards conflict.
In the story, an archboutefeu whispers lies to set the city on fire.

senses.definition: Someone who is the most foolish or ridiculous in a group, often behaving in a silly way to make people laugh.
examples: Far from being a leader, he became the archbuffoon of the committee.
The film portrays the king as an archbuffoon, constantly making absurd mistakes.

senses.definition: A chief or most important builder, especially used figuratively for someone who plays a major role in creating or shaping something.
examples: She is regarded as the archbuilder of the modern welfare state.
The company’s founder was the archbuilder of its global network.

senses.definition: The strongest or most active supporter of a person, idea, or cause.
examples: He became the archchampion of human rights in the region.
The minister is the archchampion of tax reform in the cabinet.

senses.definition: A chief or senior chaplain, especially in a royal court or large institution, mainly in historical or formal use.
examples: The king’s archchaplain advised him on both spiritual and political matters.
As archchaplain, she coordinated the work of dozens of local chaplains.

senses.definition: Someone who is a very great cheat or fake, especially a person who pretends to have skills or knowledge that they do not have.
examples: The so-called healer was exposed as an archcharlatan.
Many saw the investor as an archcharlatan who tricked people out of their savings.

senses.definition: Someone who cheats others in a major or repeated way.
examples: The court called him an archcheater who had deceived thousands of customers.
In the story, the archcheater finally faces justice.

senses.definition: Relating to the highest or most powerful form of alchemy or chemistry, mainly in old or literary use.
examples: The poem speaks of an archchemic fire that purifies the soul.
He dreamed of discovering some archchemic secret that would turn base metals into gold.

senses.definition: The highest or most important chief or leader, mainly in literary or rhetorical use.
examples: As archchief of the clan, he settled all major disputes.
The rebels swore loyalty to their archchief alone.

senses.definition: The most important or leading writer of historical records or stories.
examples: He became the archchronicler of life in the modern city.
The novelist is often seen as the archchronicler of that troubled decade.

senses.definition: A leading or main city, especially the most important city in a region or system.
examples: The capital grew into the archcity of the whole empire.
As the archcity of trade, it attracted merchants from every continent.

senses.definition: Plural of archconfraternity: several chief or principal religious brotherhoods or associations, especially in the Roman Catholic tradition.
examples: Archconfraternities played a major role in caring for the poor in many cities.
The report describes how the archconfraternities were organized and funded.

senses.definition: A chief or principal religious brotherhood, often one with special privileges or authority over similar groups.
examples: The archconfraternity was responsible for organizing major processions.
Membership in the archconfraternity was seen as a great honor.

senses.definition: Having very strong conservative political or social opinions, not wanting change at all.
examples: The senator is known for his archconservative views on social issues.
An archconservative newspaper attacked the new policies.

senses.definition: A person with very strong conservative political or social opinions.
examples: Archconservatives refused to compromise on the bill.
She is an archconservative on economic matters.

senses.definition: Plural of archconservative: people with very strong conservative political or social opinions.
examples: Archconservatives in the party opposed any change to the tax system.
The policy was praised by archconservatives but criticized by moderates.

senses.definition: Someone who is especially known for comforting and supporting others who are sad or in trouble.
examples: During the crisis, she became the archconsoler of the whole community.
He acted as archconsoler to families who had lost loved ones.

senses.definition: The main person who plans or leads a secret and often illegal plan against a government or organization.
examples: Police identified him as the archconspirator behind the plot.
The archconspirator managed to escape before the others were arrested.

senses.definition: Someone who is a leading source of moral or political corruption.
examples: The journalist called the businessman an archcorrupter of public life.
History remembers him as an archcorrupter who bought influence at every level.

senses.definition: A chief or most feared pirate or sea robber, especially in a historical or literary setting.
examples: The archcorsair ruled the seas for decades.
Legends grew up around the archcorsair and his hidden treasure.

senses.definition: A count of the highest rank among counts, mainly in historical or invented titles.
examples: The archcount controlled more land than many dukes.
In the fantasy novel, the archcount holds both military and magical power.

senses.definition: A chief or very skilled trickster who cheats or deceives people, mainly in older or literary use.
examples: He was known in the city as an archcozener of the simple and the desperate.
The play features an archcozener who fools everyone until the final scene.

senses.definition: A very dangerous or important criminal, often the leader of many crimes.
examples: The police finally captured the archcriminal who ran the gang.
In the comic book, an archcriminal threatens the entire city.

senses.definition: Someone who is a main or very strong critic of a person, idea, or plan.
examples: She is the archcritic of the government’s education policy.
The writer became an archcritic of consumer culture.

senses.definition: A rare or literary term for a supreme crown or highest royal authority.
examples: The archcrown passed from one dynasty to another.
In the legend, whoever wore the archcrown ruled all the kingdoms.

senses.definition: A chief cupbearer, especially in a royal court, who serves drinks and may be close to the ruler, mainly in historical or literary contexts.
examples: The archcupbearer tasted every drink before presenting it to the king.
As archcupbearer, she heard many secrets whispered at the royal table.

senses.definition: Written abbreviation for archduke, a high-ranking noble title below a king, used especially in historical contexts.
examples: The letter was signed by Archd Franz Ferdinand.
Several Archd titles were abolished after the revolution.

senses.definition: A historical title for a chief steward or official in charge of serving food at a royal or noble court.
examples: The archdapifer supervised the preparation of every royal feast.
As archdapifer, he controlled access to the king’s table.

senses.definition: The office, rank, or period of service of an archdapifer.
examples: His archdapifership lasted through three reigns.
The privileges of the archdapifership were carefully recorded.

senses.definition: A senior priest in some Christian churches, just below a bishop, who helps manage a large church area and its clergy.
examples: The archdeacon visited each parish in his district twice a year.
She was appointed archdeacon after many years of parish work.
The archdeacon is responsible for the churches in the eastern part of the diocese.

senses.definition: The office or administrative area of an archdeacon.
examples: She worked across the whole archdeaconate, training local church leaders.
The boundaries of the archdeaconate were changed in the nineteenth century.

senses.definition: A female archdeacon, or in some traditions, a woman with a senior church role similar to an archdeacon.
examples: The church celebrated the appointment of its first archdeaconess.
In early Christianity, the archdeaconess cared for women in the community.

senses.definition: Plural of archdeaconry: several church districts under the care of archdeacons.
examples: The diocese is divided into three archdeaconries.
Archdeaconries vary in size depending on the number of parishes.

senses.definition: A district of a diocese that an archdeacon is responsible for, or the office of an archdeacon.
examples: He oversaw the western archdeaconry of the diocese.
The archdeaconry was created in the twelfth century.
She moved to a new post in another archdeaconry.

senses.definition: Plural of archdeacon: several senior priests who help manage large church areas under a bishop.
examples: The bishop met with his archdeacons to discuss the new plan.
Archdeacons often support local clergy with practical advice.

senses.definition: The position, duties, or period of service of an archdeacon.
examples: His archdeaconship was marked by major reforms.
She resigned her archdeaconship to take up a university post.

senses.definition: A chief dean or senior official in charge of a group of deans, mainly in historical or institutional contexts.
examples: The archdean coordinated the work of all the college deans.
As archdean, he chaired important disciplinary hearings.

senses.definition: The area or office under the authority of an archdean.
examples: The archdeanery included several colleges across the city.
He reformed the rules of the archdeanery to make them clearer.

senses.definition: Someone who is extremely skilled at lying and tricking people.
examples: Many saw the dictator as an archdeceiver who lied to stay in power.
The novel’s villain is an archdeceiver who fools everyone until the end.

senses.definition: The strongest or main supporter and protector of a person, idea, or system.
examples: She is the archdefender of free speech in the university.
The judge became an archdefender of the constitution.

senses.definition: A very powerful or chief demon, especially in religious stories, myths, or fantasy fiction.
examples: The hero must defeat the archdemon to save the world.
In the legend, an archdemon tempts people with promises of power.

senses.definition: Someone who leads or is mainly responsible for serious acts of plunder, destruction, or abuse.
examples: The warlord was described as an archdepredator of the borderlands.
Environmentalists called the company an archdepredator of the rainforest.
